input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-04-03T01:16:09.383Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML — use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1C1C1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads the device's current `AppTheme` and picks `Light` or `Dark` automatically — **no event wiring needed**. It also updates live if the user toggles the system theme.

---

**C# equivalent — `SetAppTheme` extension method:**

```csharp
// In your page constructor or handler:
this.SetAppTheme(
    ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White,        // light
    Color.FromArgb("#1C1C1E")); // dark

myLabel.SetAppTheme(
    Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black,
    Colors.White);

myButton.SetAppTheme(
    Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
```

`SetAppTheme(BindableProperty, lightValue, darkValue)` does the same thing as the XAML markup extension.

---

**Key points:**

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Reacts automatically** | Bound to `Application.RequestedTheme`; updates at runtime when the OS theme changes. |
| **Works with any `BindableProperty`** | Colors, images, strings, thickness — anything bindable. |
| **Resource dictionaries** | For larger apps, define `Light`/`Dark` colors in `AppThemeBinding` inside `App.xaml` resources to keep pages clean. |
| **Force a theme** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;` to override the system choice. |
